name: l0_entry_size_overflow_check
reason: `L0_ENTRY_SIZE = 512 * 512 * L2_ENTRY_SIZE = 512^3 * 4096 = 549755813888` which exceeds `usize::MAX` on 32-bit but fits in 64-bit — tests that the constant computation doesn't overflow given `size_of usize == 8`

name: entry_sizes_strictly_decreasing
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_sizes_strictly_decreasing()
    ensures
        x86_arch_spec.layers[0].entry_size > x86_arch_spec.layers[1].entry_size,
        x86_arch_spec.layers[1].entry_size > x86_arch_spec.layers[2].entry_size,
        x86_arch_spec.layers[2].entry_size > x86_arch_spec.layers[3].entry_size,
{
}
reason: Entry sizes are strictly decreasing from L0 to L3 — tests that the layer ordering matches the expected top-down hierarchy (L0=512G, L1=1G, L2=2M, L3=4K)

name: arch_exec_fixed_size_array
reason: `ArchExec` uses `[ArchLayerExec; 4]` so every `ArchExec` instance always has exactly 4 layers — prevents representing 5-level paging (LA57) or non-standard page table depths
